title: Such a Long Journey
author: Rohinton Mistry
description: It is Bombay in 1971, the year India went to war over what was to become Bangladesh. A hard-working bank clerk, Gustad Noble is a devoted family man who gradually sees his modest life unravelling. His young daughter falls ill; his promising son defies his father’s ambitions for him. He is the one reasonable voice amidst the ongoing dramas of his neighbours. One day, he receives a letter from an old friend, asking him to help in what at first seems like an heroic mission. But he soon finds himsel...
subjects: 18.07 English literature outside Europe and the USA
subject_facet: 18.07 English literature outside Europe and the USA
